name: SOC 2 Type II — Trust Service Criteria
disclaimer: This provides technical guidance for SOC 2 compliance controls. It is NOT a SOC 2 audit and does not constitute certification. Engage a licensed CPA firm for formal SOC 2 examination.

requirements.name: Logical Access Security
requirements.description: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures to protect information assets

requirements.name: Role-Based Access
requirements.description: The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access based on roles

requirements.name: System Boundaries
requirements.description: The entity restricts access to system boundaries, including network infrastructure

requirements.name: Configuration Management
requirements.description: The entity monitors and detects configuration changes

requirements.name: Monitoring
requirements.description: The entity monitors system components for anomalies

requirements.name: Incident Response
requirements.description: The entity evaluates identified events and determines whether they constitute incidents

requirements.name: Confidential Information Identification
requirements.description: The entity identifies and maintains confidential information

checklist.text: Logical access security software and architectures protect information assets
New access is provisioned and removed based on authorization
Role-based access authorization, modification, and removal
System boundaries and network infrastructure restrictions
Data transmitted over networks is protected
Infrastructure and software change authorization and implementation

questions.question: What types of {terminology.sensitive_data} does your system process, store, or transmit?
questions.examples: customer data, financial records, authentication credentials, API keys

questions.question: Which Trust Service Criteria are in scope? (Security is always required; Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, Privacy are optional)
What sub-service organizations do you rely on? For each, do you have a contractual agreement covering security responsibilities?
